title: So How Will Occidental Audiences See It
display_title: So How Will Occidental Audiences See It
wikitext:
[]: So, How Will Occidental Audiences See It?             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Dec 23, 2006 10:49 pm Rebuild comes out in Japan starting summer of 2007, but how will fans in the West see it? Will we all have to wait until it's licensed/fansubbed? I'd love it if it were shown subtitled in limited American theaters in Japanese areas (a la the original Godzilla, and not the edited-for-America version), but I doubt that'll happen. The second best thing that I could think of doing (though it's also quite unlikely) is to find a translated script, and I'd try to fly to Japan and read the script along with the movie in theater... unlikely not only because of getting a translated script, but because I don't know Japanese and would have no idea what the frak I'm supposed to do once I arrive. So, I'm assuming the actual summer 2007 release is not how I or most other people in my area will see it. The question is, then, how? :?

[]: Re: So, How Will Occidental Audiences See It?             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Dec 23, 2006 11:26 pm NAveryW wrote: Rebuild comes out in Japan starting summer of 2007, but how will fans in the West see it? Will we all have to wait until it's licensed/fansubbed? I'd love it if it were shown subtitled in limited American theaters in Japanese areas (a la the original Godzilla, and not the edited-for-America version), but I doubt that'll happen. The second best thing that I could think of doing (though it's also quite unlikely) is to find a translated script, and I'd try to fly to Japan and read the script along with the movie in theater... unlikely not only because of getting a translated script, but because I don't know Japanese and would have no idea what the frak I'm supposed to do once I arrive. So, I'm assuming the actual summer 2007 release is not how I or most other people in my area will see it. The question is, then, how? :?

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Sat Dec 23, 2006 11:41 pm Does it make me an asshole that I'll just wait until a fansub torrent is available then download it?

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Sun Dec 24, 2006 12:23 am Yeah, odds are that there will be a fansub release followed by an ADV DVD release a year later. ADV has played with doing theatrical releases before for unremarkable fillms like the live-action Princess Blade , so it's possible they might try it again (even though that release was during the anime expansion bubble that popped a while back). However, I see a zero probability of simultaneous release, mainly because ADV would feel compelled to dub the thing first.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Dec 24, 2006 11:40 am Rights-wise, I'm almost hoping a group like Geneon (Pioneer) (did a decent job on Sailor Moon S and SuperS (unlike ADV with season 1 & R!), so they're okay in my book) or rightstuf (seem to be a company that's for fans, by fans) gets it, but it'll probably be ADV (as long as it's not Mangle, okay! ) I'd just gonna wait until it's uploaded via bittorrent then watch it that way. Fansubbed or raw, I don't care. I WANNA WATCH SOME NEW EVA! :twisted:

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Dec 24, 2006 12:51 pm ADV said that they didn't get the Eva movies because GAINAX was asking for a very large amount of money for the license that ADV wasn't willing to pay, but Manga was. So, Manga got the license. Now, for this release, I wouldn't be surprised if GAINAX asks for even more money. Because, hey, SOMEBODY'S going to buy it.

[]:             Quote Post by Millions Livio » Sun Dec 24, 2006 2:31 pm I'll download the raw off bittorrent when it comes up, which will proabably be within two days of it's release. Then the fansubs when they come out. That might sound pointless to some, but I still got the gist of the Hellsing OVA episode one before the fansubs came out. It's not too hard to piece together what is going on if the voice actors are good.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Dec 24, 2006 8:29 pm Knowing even some nihongo helps, too. I think it would be really cool if the movies would be released in theaters, subbed and hell even dubbed like what happened with that Ghost in the Shell 2 movie.

[]:             Quote Post by brainDamage » Sun Dec 24, 2006 9:22 pm I'm guessing that it will come out on DVD to America shortly after it does in Japan. I doubt we will get an actual theatrical release.

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Sun Dec 24, 2006 9:39 pm So, How Will Occidental Audiences See It?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Dec 24, 2006 10:38 pm MaggotMaster wrote: So, How Will Occidental Audiences See It?

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Sun Dec 24, 2006 10:54 pm NAveryW wrote: MaggotMaster wrote: So, How Will Occidental Audiences See It?

[]:             Quote Post by Six Winged Angel » Mon Dec 25, 2006 3:06 am naaah, it will come to fansub like Advent Children,...few hours laters :twisted:

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Dec 25, 2006 11:54 am But aahh... since it's being released theatrically, won't the only fansub available be a shaky camcorded video of the movie screen?

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Mon Dec 25, 2006 3:31 pm NAveryW wrote: But aahh... since it's being released theatrically, won't the only fansub available be a shaky camcorded video of the movie screen?

[]:             Quote Post by master0rolando » Tue Dec 26, 2006 3:54 am i give it about 5minuites before its subbed... someone will probly get their crap rigged up to subb it WHILE it plays in theaters...

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Tue Dec 26, 2006 4:07 am master0rolando wrote: i give it about 5minuites before its subbed... someone will probly get their crap rigged up to subb it WHILE it plays in theaters...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Dec 29, 2006 3:38 pm http://lazytorrents.ytmnd.com/ Glad I know some Japanese, just in case the early fansubs (you know more than one group is gonna do it; screw efficiency) suck ass.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Dec 29, 2006 4:53 pm Reichu wrote: http://lazytorrents.ytmnd.com/ Glad I know some Japanese, just in case the early fansubs (you know more than one group is gonna do it; screw efficiency) suck ass.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Sat Dec 30, 2006 8:41 am What can i say? I live in Europe!
